name: Farmer Carry
description: Walk holding heavy dumbbells (or kettlebells) at the sides. Stand tall — ribs down, shoulders pulled DOWN and BACK (not shrugged toward ears), neck long, eyes forward. Walk in a straight line at a deliberate pace, breathing steadily. The goal is to keep the shoulder packed under load — traps engage tonically to support the joint, which is the same pattern that resolves chronic upper-trap tightness. Common cue: 'tall posture, packed shoulders, breathe.' Distance/time-based: 30-60s per set is typical for shoulder/posture work; longer walks (60-90s) when grip is the focus. Use heavy enough that grip is challenged by the end but posture stays clean.
